IN HIS OWN RIGHT.Definition: because of his own achievements, not someone else's

ANTI-SLAVERY ACTIVIST.Definition: a person who worked to end slavery

DOMESTIC WORKER.Definition: a person who worked in someone's home doing jobs such as cleaning or cooking

BY ALL ACCOUNTS.Definition: according to everyone who knew about it

MODEST.Definition: simple and not expensive or grand

SECOND-CLASS CITIZENS.Definition: people who were treated as less important than others

FOLKLORE.Definition: traditional stories that many people know and tell

PRIDE AND JOY.Definition: something he loved very much and was very proud of

HOOKED.Definition: very interested in it and wanting to keep doing it

DAWN.Definition: the time when the sun started to rise

SKIPPED ROPE.Definition: jumped over a rope again and again for exercise

ON PAPER.Definition: according to the facts or information available

AMATEUR.Definition: doing the sport for enjoyment, not for money

LOGISTICAL.Definition: connected with organising and planning things

PARACHUTE.Definition: a device that helps a person fall safely from an aircraft

SCORE A POINT.Definition: make someone else look wrong or make himself look right

RACIAL POLITICS.Definition: issues and arguments connected with race and society

QUALIFIED.Definition: having the skills or knowledge needed

KNOCKOUT BLOW.Definition: a punch that would make him unable to continue fighting

STEADY HIMSELF.Definition: make himself balanced and in control again

UNCONVINCED.Definition: not believing that it was the right way

FOUND OUT.Definition: shown to be weaker or worse than people thought

TECHNICALLY GIFTED.Definition: very skilled at the technical parts

PERSONA.Definition: the character or image he showed to others

PEROXIDE BLONDE.Definition: very light blonde hair made with chemicals

FLAMBOYANCE.Definition: a very colourful and attention-seeking style

ARROGANT.Definition: believing you are better than other people

TEMPLATE.Definition: an example that could be copied

BOOKMAKERS.Definition: people or companies that took bets on sports

OVERWHELMING FAVOURITE.Definition: the person expected to win very easily

ANTAGONISTIC.Definition: unfriendly and likely to argue or cause conflict

RINGSIDE SEAT.Definition: a seat very close to the boxing ring

MAINSTREAM.Definition: accepted or followed by most people

OPPRESSION.Definition: unfair and cruel treatment by people with power

PREACHED.Definition: publicly taught or strongly supported the idea

SEPARATISM.Definition: the belief that they should live separately from others

SELF-SUFFICIENCY.Definition: the ability to provide what they needed without help

RADICAL.Definition: very different from what most people accepted, extreme

SELF-DEFINITION.Definition: deciding and declaring who he was

COMPLIANT.Definition: willing to do what others want

MILITARY DRAFT.Definition: a system that forced people to join the armed forces

CONSCIENTIOUS OBJECTOR.Definition: a person who refused military service because of his beliefs

DRAFT BOARD.Definition: the group that decided who had to join the military

RAMIFICATIONS.Definition: results or effects, especially serious ones

SENTENCED.Definition: officially given a punishment by a court

BAIL.Definition: money or an agreement that allowed him to stay out of prison before trial

APPEALED.Definition: waiting for a final decision from a higher court

HANGING OVER HIS HEAD.Definition: causing worry because it could happen

TRAITOR.Definition: a person who was seen as betraying his country

PRIME.Definition: best and strongest period of his life

CONVICTION.Definition: an official decision that he was guilty of a crime

SLIPPED.Definition: avoided by moving quickly out of the way

ACTIVIST.Definition: a person who worked to bring about change

ESTABLISHMENT.Definition: the people and groups that held power in society

PUNCHERS.Definition: boxers known for hitting very hard

ROPES.Definition: the ropes around a boxing ring

COVER UP.Definition: protect himself by keeping his arms and gloves in front of him

TACTICAL.Definition: relating to a carefully planned way of doing it

PARKINSON'S SYNDROME.Definition: a disease that makes movement and speaking more difficult

NEUROLOGICAL.Definition: relating to the brain and nerves

IRREVERENT.Definition: showing little respect for things people usually respect

CAULDRON.Definition: a large metal container; at the Olympics, the large bowl that holds the flame

INTAKE OF BREATH.Definition: a sudden breath caused by surprise or emotion

LIVELIHOOD.Definition: the way he earned money to live

SECOND ACTS.Definition: successful new periods after an earlier success

WITH A STRAIGHT FACE.Definition: without showing any shock or facial reaction
